lines.en_lit: But isn’t there the limbs and the fats? is the end of the daytime service. But isn’t there the removal of the ashes? the start of the daytime service, Rabbi Yoḥanan said: sanctified his hands for the removal of the ashes, the next day, he need not sanctify as he already sanctified at the start of the service. If so, difficult.
Rather, when it was stated, this is how it was stated: Rabbi Asi said Rabbi Yoḥanan said: A non-priest who arranges the two logs is liable since it is a service that is complete,

Rava strongly objects to this: However, if so, should require a lottery. And doesn’t it require a lottery? Wasn’t it taught Whoever was privileged removal of the ashes also privileged arrangement of the two logs?
this is what said: they hold a lottery for it by itself. as we said

Is that to say that a complete service and a non-priest liable the death penalty requires a lottery, a non-priest not liable the death penalty it would not require a lottery? But isn’t there slaughtering, Slaughtering is different, it is the beginning of the daytime service,

Is that to say that a service that is complete requires a lottery, a service which is followed by a service does not require a lottery? But isn’t there the limbs and the fats? is the end of the daytime service. Isn’t there the removal of the ashes? due to the incident that occurred

Mar Zutra, and some say Rav Ashi, said: We too have learned The appointed said to them: Go out and see if the time for slaughtering has arrived, whereas did not teach: the time for arranging the two logs
teach that which has no rectification It does not teach something that has rectification

MISHNA: The appointed said to Go out and observe if the time for slaughter arrived. If arrived, the observer says: There is light [barkai]. Matya ben Shmuel says Is the entire eastern sky illuminated even to Hebron? And says: Yes. And why did they need to as once, the light of the moon rose, and they imagined that the eastern was illuminated and they slaughtered the daily offering taken out to the place burning

they led the High Priest down to the Hall of Immersion. This was the principle in the Temple: Anyone who covers his legs, requires immersion and anyone who urinates requires sanctification of the hands and feet
